directions.name: baseline-handcrafted-yaml
directions.description: Direct 1:1 structural mapping of BSV constructs to YAML fields (bsv_typedef). Template-based Python compiler (no LLM).
directions.verdict: Compiles but no abstraction; language-dependent; YAML larger than BSV

directions.name: declarative-yaml-llm-compiler
directions.description: Declarative natural-language YAML descriptions + LLM-based BSV generation.
directions.verdict: Functionally correct but YAML 3.75x larger than BSV

directions.name: pure-declarative-no-logic
directions.description: YAML only describes structure (WHAT): interfaces, ports, sub-modules, connections. Zero behavioral logic. LLM infers everything.
directions.verdict: BREAKTHROUGH: YAML more compact than BSV. Proves graph+intent→RTL hypothesis.

directions.name: structural-plus-hints
directions.description: Hybrid: structural YAML (from iter_003) + minimal algorithmic hints for complex logic like round-robin arbitration.
directions.verdict: Extends pure-declarative to algorithmic modules. ID<1.0 maintained.

directions.name: two-phase-compiler
directions.description: Two-Phase compiler strategy: Phase 1 generates type definitions from YAML, Phase 2 generates function/module code.
directions.result: Phase 1: FPC=1.0, ID=0.20, AL=0.75, LIS=0. Phase 2: pending.
directions.verdict: Two-Phase works for types. Enum/struct declarations are inherently compact in YAML.

unused_strategies: change compiler generation strategy (Bottom-Up vs Top-Down)
change knowledge template granularity (merge/split knowledge levels)
change connection model (explicit wires vs implicit inference)
change natural language description style (algorithmic vs behavioral)
